comment_text.sn14.35:1.2: Said also of the aggregates (SN 22.29) and the senses (SN 35.19, 20).

root_text.sn14.35:1.2: “Yo, bhikkhave, pathavīdhātuṁ abhinandati, dukkhaṁ so abhinandati.
root_text.sn14.35:1.3: Yo dukkhaṁ abhinandati, ‘aparimutto so dukkhasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn14.35:1.6: yo vāyodhātuṁ abhinandati, dukkhaṁ so abhinandati.
root_text.sn14.35:1.7: Yo dukkhaṁ abhinandati, ‘aparimutto so dukkhasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn14.35:2.1: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, pathavīdhātuṁ nābhinandati, dukkhaṁ so nābhinandati.
root_text.sn14.35:2.2: Yo dukkhaṁ nābhinandati, ‘parimutto so dukkhasmā’ti vadāmi.
root_text.sn14.35:2.5: yo vāyodhātuṁ nābhinandati, dukkhaṁ so nābhinandati.
root_text.sn14.35:2.6: Yo dukkhaṁ nābhinandati, ‘parimutto so dukkhasmā’ti vadāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn14.35:1.2: “Mendicants, if you take pleasure in the earth element, you take pleasure in suffering.
translation_text.sn14.35:1.3: If you take pleasure in suffering, you’re not free from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn14.35:1.4: If you take pleasure in the water element …
translation_text.sn14.35:1.5: If you take pleasure in the fire element …
translation_text.sn14.35:1.6: If you take pleasure in the air element, you take pleasure in suffering.
translation_text.sn14.35:1.7: If you take pleasure in suffering, you’re not free from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn14.35:2.1: If you don’t take pleasure in the earth element, you don’t take pleasure in suffering.
translation_text.sn14.35:2.2: If you don’t take pleasure in suffering, you’re free from suffering, I say.
translation_text.sn14.35:2.3: If you don’t take pleasure in the water element …
translation_text.sn14.35:2.4: If you don’t take pleasure in the fire element …
translation_text.sn14.35:2.5: If you don’t take pleasure in the air element, you don’t take pleasure in suffering.
translation_text.sn14.35:2.6: If you don’t take pleasure in suffering, you’re free from suffering, I say.”
